key_insight: Autogenous CO₂ laser welding can produce sound joints in 2 mm Strenx 700 steel while creating a narrow, highly hardened fusion zone dominated by martensite, bainite, and Widmanstätten ferrite.
engineering_relevance: For lightweight agricultural machinery, laser welding offers a promising route to preserve high strength and potentially extend fatigue life versus conventional arc welding, but the weld microstructure must be tightly controlled.
method: Autogenous laser welding of 2 mm Strenx 700 sheets at 1100, 1300, and 1500 W
Parallel-edge joints with no root opening to reduce process complexity and cost
Post-weld macro/microstructural analysis, microhardness mapping, tensile testing, and tension-tension fatigue testing
evidence_signal: The abstract reports satisfactory weld formation and detailed zone-specific microstructures, with performance assessed using microscopy, hardness, tensile, and uniaxial fatigue evaluations.
watch_item: The study is based on manual welds and a limited parameter set, so process robustness and industrial repeatability still need validation.
paper_title: Effects of Laser Welding on the Microstructure and Fatigue Behavior of Strenx 700 CR Steel
